title: Upgrading from Kubic Ubuntu packages overwrites containers.conf
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I'm using Podman in WSL2 and have to change the containers.conf not to use systemd/journald after upgrades to make it work.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Upgrade to newer  Podman version after containers.conf is changed


**Describe the results you received:**

containers.conf is overwritten by new package version

**Describe the results you expected:**

Config should stay the same or ask the user to merge the new config.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

This happend with upgrade to 3.0.1-2

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**



**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

WSL2

comments.body:
Which containers.conf - in /usr/share or /etc? It is intended that the one
in usr/share be overwritten. User changes should be made in /etc/containers.

On Fri, Feb 26, 2021 at 12:34 c-goes <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)*
>
> /kind bug
>
> *Description*
>
> I'm using Podman in WSL2 and have to change the containers.conf not to use
> systemd/journald after upgrades to make it work.

> *Describe the results you expected:*

> Config should stay the same or ask the user to merge the new config.

> occasionally):*

> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9530>, or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCBWGND3FTWOK6UNURDTA7LQNANCNFSM4YI2M4RA>
> .
>

> Which containers.conf - in /usr/share or /etc? It is intended that the one in usr/share be overwritten. User changes should be made in /etc/containers.

I just noticed that on my system /etc/containers/containers.conf is a symlink to /usr/share/containers/containers.conf (probably created from an older version of the package). I didn't expect this.
This symlinking isn't done anymore since  October or November. Thus, for new installations this should work now as expected. I ran `apt purge` on all the containers packages, removed the symlinks and reinstalled and it's working now.
